lead.title: Thursday, 14 May 2026
lead.standfirst: President Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping held a two-day summit in Beijing — attended by top American CEOs including Elon Musk, Tim Cook, and Larry Fink — covering trade, the Iran war, and Taiw.

secondary.title: Cloudflare Cuts 1,100 Jobs at Record Revenue
secondary.standfirst: Cloudflare announced its first mass layoff in sixteen years on May 7 — 1,100 jobs eliminated alongside record quarterly revenue — and explicitly attributed the cut to internal AI agent adoption. The move is the clearest disclosed case yet of AI displacement at a growing company.

secondary.title: Wall Street's AI Chip Rotation
secondary.standfirst: AMD, Intel, and Micron have outrun Nvidia by orders of magnitude year-to-date as Wall Street rotates AI-hardware capital from training GPUs to inference-era CPUs and memory. Nvidia's counter-move is a 0 billion equity-investment campaign across its own customer base.

secondary.title: Warsh Clears Cloture as Partisan Fed Confirmation Concludes
secondary.standfirst: The Senate's Monday cloture vote on Kevin Warsh's Fed nomination — 49-44 on party lines — caps the most strictly partisan confirmation process in Fed Chair history. Warsh inherits a Fed pinned between an Iran-war energy shock and a shutdown-distorted core inflation print due Tuesday.

further_reading.title: Hormuz Under Fire
further_reading.standfirst: US fires on Iranian tankers as Hormuz talks stall; ceasefire holds nominally. A US Navy F/A-18 disabled two Iranian-flagged tankers attempting to breach the naval blockade. Iran has yet to respond to a 14-point MOU proposed by Washington.

further_reading.title: Trade Court Strikes Down Trump's Section 122 Tariffs
further_reading.standfirst: A 2-1 panel of the US Court of International Trade ruled on May 7 that the administration's 10% global import surcharge exceeds presidential authority under Section 122 of the Trade Act of 1974 — but limited relief to three named plaintiffs, leaving the surcharge in place for all other US importers.

further_reading.title: China's Legal Shield
further_reading.standfirst: China's Ministry of Commerce invoked its Blocking Rules for the first time on 2 May, ordering domestic firms not to comply with US sanctions on five Chinese oil refineries. The legal escalation transforms a bilateral diplomatic dispute into a multilateral compliance problem for any international bank or shipper caught between Washington and Beijing.

further_reading.title: White House Reverses on AI Oversight
further_reading.standfirst: An administration that spent its first year opposing AI oversight is now drafting an executive order requiring pre-deployment government vetting of advanced models, following the Mythos security alarm. The reversal signals that national security framing is doing what the broader AI safety debate failed to do.

further_reading.title: The Pharma Tariff Tier
further_reading.standfirst: Trump's Section 232 pharmaceutical tariffs impose 100% on patented drug imports from 17 named companies, with a July 31 deadline. The tiered structure — 100% default, 20% for approved onshoring plans, zero for MFN holders — creates a compliance landscape almost entirely absent from mainstream coverage.

further_reading.title: Trump-Xi Beijing Summit Opens Under Iran Shadow
further_reading.standfirst: Trump arrived in Beijing on May 12 for a state visit whose formal agenda — tariffs, rare earths, Taiwan, AI — has been crowded by an Iran ceasefire Trump himself called 'on massive life support,' with Xi holding more diplomatic leverage on Tehran than the White House had planned for.

further_reading.title: April CPI Reaccelerates to 3.8% as Energy Shock Hits Headline
further_reading.standfirst: Headline CPI rose to 3.8% in April — the highest since May 2023 — driven by a 17.9% annual energy surge, but core inflation also reaccelerated to 2.8%, complicating the Fed's three-meeting hold and the White House's push for cuts.

further_reading.title: Victory Day Ceasefire Expires; Putin Floats Third-Country Meeting
further_reading.standfirst: The US-brokered three-day Victory Day ceasefire expired May 11 with mutual violation claims, even as the 1,000-for-1,000 prisoner swap proceeded and Putin offered, conditionally, to meet Zelenskyy in a third country once a long-term treaty is in place.
